expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the Lean Gain honesty actionability question in JSON and Markdown
the fixture exposes benchmarkScoreboard.primary with benchmark scope, baseline, method, remaining-percent metrics, and reported-change metrics
the fixture marks currentRepoBoundary.perRepoSavingsClaim as not-computed with a reason
the fixture routes current-repo evidence back to lean audit, lean review, and lean debt
the Markdown exposes Benchmark Scoreboard, Honesty Boundary, Current Repo Signals, and no-claim language
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
privateDataPolicy: This promoted fixture is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm benchmark direction is separate from current-repo savings.
Confirm current-repo savings remain not-computed without a matched baseline.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.

publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard Lean Gain report pair that proves benchmark-backed impact remains separate from current-repo savings claims.
sourceQuestion: Does lean gain avoid fake per-repo savings while still showing benchmark-backed impact?
